basis: reopens adult juvenile-like (critical-period) plasticity and promotes hippocampal neurogenesis + BDNF/TrkB signalling (iPlasticity); induced visual-cortex plasticity can outlast the natural critical period and some cellular dematuration is reported after withdrawal in rodents
caveat: a PERMISSIVE window - durable change is contingent on paired experience/training (e.g. fluoxetine + extinction), not a standalone boost; rodent work at supra-clinical doses; no human cognitive-enhancement persistence shown
source: Maya-Vetencourt 2008 Science; Steinzeig 2019 Eur J Neurosci; Castren iPlasticity reviews

basis: SNRI antidepressant; mood/secondary effects reverse on washout; no established durable cognitive enhancement
caveat: routed to catecholaminergic by aryloxypropanamine scaffold; symptomatic

basis: MAO-B inhibitor; the DATATOP / Sano lineage delayed functional endpoints in AD/PD but did not durably enhance cognition
caveat: disease-course modification not established; functional not cognitive endpoints

basis: ADAGIO delayed-start: rasagiline 1 mg/day met all three hierarchical endpoints (consistent with a disease-modifying effect) but 2 mg/day did not - a dose-inconsistency that undercuts interpretation; endpoints were motor (UPDRS), cognition was a tiny mentation sub-score
caveat: equivocal; motor not cognitive; field consensus is that MAO-B inhibitors have not clearly altered Parkinson's course

basis: quaternary-ammonium AChE inhibitor; permanent positive charge means it does not cross the blood-brain barrier (used for myasthenia and reversal of neuromuscular blockade)
caveat: no central cognitive action regardless of scaffold similarity to galantamine; pure routing artifact

basis: bis-quaternary AChE inhibitor; BBB-impermeant
bis-quaternary AChE inhibitor used as an ophthalmic glaucoma agent; BBB-impermeant
peripherally-restricted kappa-opioid agonist (uremic pruritus), engineered not to cross the BBB
mu-opioid agonist; chronic opioid exposure is associated with cognitive impairment plus tolerance and dependence
caveat: safety inversion - potential lasting harm, not enhancement

basis: mu-opioid agonist; chronic use impairs cognition
mu-opioid agonist; chronic use impairs cognition
mu-opioid agonist; chronic use impairs cognition
antimuscarinic antispasmodic; chronic anticholinergic burden is itself linked to higher dementia risk
source: anticholinergic cognitive-burden literature (Gray 2015 JAMA Intern Med)

basis: sedating first-generation antihistamine with anticholinergic activity
caveat: sedating - the opposite of wake-promoting; the 'wake_promoting' label here is structural not pharmacological

basis: antiepileptic carrying a black-box warning (aplastic anaemia / hepatic failure); not a wake-promoting agent
NSAID; the ADAPT RCT of naproxen/celecoxib did not improve cognition (weak detrimental signal for naproxen); ibufenac itself was withdrawn for hepatotoxicity
caveat: contested AD-prevention hypothesis that failed its RCT; not enhancement; withdrawn drug

basis: NSAID; ADAPT RCT negative for cognitive protection
caveat: contested prevention hypothesis, failed RCT; not enhancement

basis: topical antifungal (tinea versicolor); not a systemic CNS agent
class-IB antiarrhythmic (sodium-channel blocker); no cognition mechanism
obsolete short-acting muscle relaxant
antianginal, withdrawn for torsades risk
selective alpha-2A agonist that strengthens prefrontal working-memory circuits (Arnsten), but guanfacine-ER is in the same discontinuation dataset showing rapid relapse off-drug
caveat: mechanistically interesting yet still symptomatic; benefit reverses on stopping
source: Arnsten alpha-2A PFC work; guanfacine-ER discontinuation studies
